source: Adapted from UNRA/DUCAR prioritization practice. Weights are representative ranges -- actual weights set by decision-maker based on policy priorities.
disclaimer: REPRESENTATIVE -- these weights reflect typical practice, not mandatory values. Decision-makers should review and confirm weights for their specific context.

scoring_scale.description: 1 = lowest priority, 5 = highest priority for each criterion

criteria.description: Current pavement condition from Skill #56 assessment

scoring_guide.5: Very Poor condition (VCI < 40, IRI > 8 paved / > 16 gravel)

criteria.description: Average daily traffic or estimated usage level
Benefit-cost ratio from economic appraisal (Skill #65 TARA data)
criteria.notes: Set to 0.00 if no economic appraisal data available. Redistribute weight to other criteria.

criteria.description: Population served, access to health facilities, schools, markets
Trade corridor, tourism route, border access, connectivity function

scoring_guide.1: Local access only, no strategic function
scoring_guide.3: Sub-county connectivity or minor trade route
scoring_guide.4: District connectivity or significant trade route
scoring_guide.5: International trade corridor, tourism gateway, or critical connectivity link

equity_adjusted.notes: Includes regional equity criterion to address geographic imbalance in road investment
